# omada_port_forwards (Data Source)

Lists port-forwarding rules for a site — handy for discovering rule IDs to import.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Discover port-forward rule IDs (e.g. to write import blocks).
data "omada_port_forwards" "all" {}

output "port_forward_ids" {
  value = { for r in data.omada_port_forwards.all.port_forwards : r.name => r.id }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `site` (String) Site name to query (resolved to an ID). Ignored if `site_id` is set.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID to query. Provide this or `site`; if neither is set, the provider's default site is used.

### Read-Only

- `port_forwards` (Attributes List) The port-forwarding rules defined on the site. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--port_forwards))

<a id="nestedatt--port_forwards"></a>
### Nested Schema for `port_forwards`

Read-Only:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.
- `external_port` (String) External port or range.
- `forward_ip` (String) Internal destination IP.
- `forward_port` (String) Internal destination port or range.
- `id` (String) The rule ID (use this to import `omada_port_forward`).
- `name` (String) The rule name.
- `protocol` (Number) Protocol: 1=TCP, 2=UDP, 3=TCP+UDP.
